sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Mallorca have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, managing 2 wins but suffering 3 losses, including heavy defeats to Barcelona and Atlético Madrid. Real Betis, on the other hand, have had a more stable run with 3 wins in their last 5, though they faced a significant 0-5 loss to Atlético Madrid. Betis appear to have the upper hand in terms of consistency and momentum coming into this match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these teams, Real Betis have dominated, securing victories in both matches (3-0 at home and 1-0 away). Mallorca have struggled to score against Betis, failing to find the net in either game, which highlights Betis' defensive solidity in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Mallorca, sitting 18th with 24 points, have scored 28 goals but conceded 37, showing defensive vulnerabilities. Real Betis, positioned 5th with 38 points, have a more balanced record with 37 goals scored and 28 conceded, indicating a stronger attack and defense. Betis also outperform Mallorca in key areas like possession and final third entries.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Mallorca, Vedat Muriqi (15 goals, 7.17 rating) is in fine form and will be crucial for their attack. Real Betis will rely on Antony (6 goals, 4 assists, 7.27 rating), who shone in their last head-to-head with a perfect 10 rating, to unlock Mallorca’s defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Betis' superior league position, recent form, and head-to-head dominance, they are favored to win with a 48% probability per Sofascore. Mallorca's defensive issues and inability to keep clean sheets (9-match streak) could be exploited by Betis' attacking prowess. Expect a competitive match, but Betis should edge it.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Estadi de Son Moix saw Real Betis take a commanding lead over Mallorca with a 2-0 scoreline. Real Betis struck early with a goal in the 18th minute and added a second just before the break at the 45th minute, showcasing their clinical finishing. Mallorca, despite dominating possession and creating numerous entries into the final third, failed to convert their chances into goals. The home side struggled to create big opportunities, while Real Betis made the most of their limited but dangerous attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: In terms of attack momentum, the game started with Real Betis showing early intent, pushing forward with purpose in the opening minutes. Mallorca responded with a prolonged period of pressure, maintaining consistent attacking spells through much of the first half. However, momentum shifted back to Real Betis around the time of their first goal, with a sharp attacking burst. The away side then had intermittent spells of dominance, especially leading up to their second goal late in the half. Mallorca fought to regain control towards the end but couldn't break through the Betis defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player performances
halftimeSections.text: For Real Betis, Cédric Bakambu and Abdessamad Ezzalzouli stood out with their goal-scoring contributions, proving to be constant threats upfront. Antony also played a pivotal role with an assist and dangerous play in the final third. On the Mallorca side, Sergi Darder showed creativity in midfield, while Vedat Muriqi posed a threat with his presence upfront, though without a goal to show for it. Defensively, Real Betis held firm with solid clearances from players like Diego Llorente and Natan, frustrating Mallorca's attacking efforts.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca's approach was heavily focused on maintaining possession and building play through the middle and final third, as evidenced by their higher number of entries and corner kicks. However, their inability to create clear-cut chances highlights a lack of sharpness in the box. Real Betis, on the other hand, adopted a more direct and counter-attacking style, capitalizing on quick transitions and clinical finishing. The away team's defense was well-organized, winning more duels overall, while Mallorca's aerial strength didn't translate into goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, Mallorca will likely push harder to claw back into the game, potentially taking more risks in attack. They need to convert their possession into tangible threats, with players like Muriqi and Darder key to unlocking the Betis defense. Real Betis, with a two-goal cushion, might sit deeper to protect their lead while looking for counter-attacking opportunities through Bakambu and Ezzalzouli. Discipline will be crucial for Betis, as they’ve already picked up a couple of yellow cards. Expect Mallorca to dominate possession again, but Betis could exploit any defensive lapses.
